messages.content:
⠙⠹⠸{"type":"thread.started","thread_id":"019e6e19-29da-70e1-be84-8f8746927752"}
Voy a leer el README y la estructura del repo para revisar la arquitectura en contexto, luego te devuelvo hallazgos priorizados con referencias concretas.Veo un README principal y otro template público. Para “README architecture” asumo que quieres revisar el principal contra la arquitectura/documentación existente, así que voy a contrastarlo con los docs de arquitectura más relevantes y el package actual.El README es extenso y mezcla descripción pública, estado actual, blueprints futuros y detalles de catálogo. Estoy revisando si esa mezcla crea claims ambiguos o inconsistencias con lo que realmente existe en `apps/web` y `src`.**Findings**

1. **High: README claims lab network traffic does not expose secrets, but the access code is sent in request bodies.**  
   [README.md](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/README.md:1024) says Network should show `/api/lab/catalog` and `/api/lab/tool` “sin exponer secretos”. Current routes validate `body.ownerAccessCode`: [catalog route](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/apps/web/app/api/lab/catalog/route.ts:19), [tool route](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/apps/web/app/api/lab/tool/route.ts:26). The env secret is not exposed as `NEXT_PUBLIC`, but the typed code is visible in browser devtools/request payloads. README should either reword this validation or the app should move to a short-lived server session/cookie flow.

2. **Medium: README contradicts itself on whether the read-only preview is simulation-only.**  
   It describes a real browser `FileList` metadata preview with `simulationOnly: false` at [README.md](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/README.md:91) and [README.md](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/README.md:95), but later says everything remains `simulation-only` and there is no filesystem read at [README.md](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/README.md:120). The implementation does enable browser folder selection via `webkitdirectory`: [ControlledReadOnlyPreviewPanel.tsx](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/apps/web/app/lab/ControlledReadOnlyPreviewPanel.tsx:17). The README should define this as “implemented browser metadata-only preview, no content upload/write/delete/move,” separate from pure simulation.

3. **Medium: `/api/ai/run` is missing from the architecture/API section.**  
   README’s “API Principal” only lists `/api/v1/run`, `/api/lab/catalog`, and `/api/lab/tool`: [README.md](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/README.md:866). But the app has an OpenAI-backed server route at [route.ts](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/apps/web/app/api/ai/run/route.ts:1), calling `runAiTask` which requires `OPENAI_API_KEY`: [ai.ts](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/apps/web/services/ai.ts:211). This should be documented with its purpose, auth/rate-limit status, failure mode when no key is configured, and how it differs from `/api/v1/run`.

4. **Low: README is carrying too much blueprint detail and is likely to drift.**  
   It duplicates large sections from dedicated docs, then links those docs at [README.md](/Users/raiderdelcastilloabalos/Documents/Proyectos/Proyecto%20UNV-HMND-fase-1%20REAL%202/README.md:1029). Architecturally, the README would be stronger as a concise “current state + safe boundaries + run/test + doc index,” with detailed blueprint material kept in `docs/MASTER_PRODUCTION_ARCHITECTURE.md` and related docs.

I did not run tests; this was a static architecture/documentation review.⠙
